subject: Cloud Computing
title: Functions as a Service
description: One-minute skill: Functions as a Service.

hook.text: What if you could run one function — not a whole server — and pay only for the milliseconds it runs?

buildup.text: FaaS lets you deploy individual functions. They wake up on an event, execute, then sleep.

discovery.text: AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, and Google Cloud Functions are the major FaaS offerings.

twist.text: Cold starts are the catch. A sleeping function takes extra time to wake up on first request.

climax.text: FaaS is ideal for event-driven tasks: image resizing, webhooks, data processing, scheduled jobs.

punchline.text: One function, one event, one bill. That's FaaS in three words.

quiz.question: What is a 'cold start' in serverless functions?
options: The delay when a sleeping function needs to initialize before handling a request
When a function runs out of memory and crashes
The process of deploying a function for the first time
